blocks:
Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمُدَّثِّرُ (۱)
O you wrapped up in your mantle! (1)
قُمْ فَأَنذِرْ (۲)
Rise up and warn! (2)
وَرَبَّكَ فَكَبِّرْ (۳)
Magnify your Lord, (3)
وَثِيَابَكَ فَطَهِّرْ (٤)
and purify your clothes, (4)
وَٱلرُّجْزَ فَٱهْجُرْ (٥)
and keep away from all impurity! (5)
وَلَا تَمْنُن تَسْتَكْثِرُ (٦)
Do not grant a favour seeking a greater gain, (6)
وَلِرَبِّكَ فَٱصْبِرْ (۷)
and be patient for the sake of your Lord. (7)

Key Points

The word "قُم" (Qum - Arise) appears twice in the Quran, both times addressed to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: one is for the night prayer and self-purification ("قم اللّیل" - Arise [for] the night), and the other is for guidance and societal reform ("قم فانذر" - Arise and warn).

Al-Muddaththir (The Enshrouded One) comes from dithār, meaning a long garment worn over other clothes, or one used to cover oneself while sleeping. (In contrast to shi‘ār, which is the inner garment.)

We observe the command "كبِّر" (Kabbir - Magnify) in the takbīrāt (declarations of greatness) of the adhān (call to prayer) and iqāmah (second call), the Takbīrat al-Ihrām (opening takbīr of prayer), and also in the tasbīhāt al-arba‘ah (fourfold glorifications) of the obligatory prayers.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of "و ثیابك فطّهر" (And your garments purify) is to shorten your clothes. This is because, during the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah), the elite wore garments so long that part of them dragged on the ground, and they hired people to carry that part along with them. And Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: The meaning of "ثیابك فطهّر" (Your garments purify) is to lift the hem of the garment to prevent it from dragging on the ground and becoming soiled.(109)

The word rujz is synonymous with rijs, meaning impurity and defilement, and is therefore used regarding every kind of sin, polytheism (shirk), idol, punishment, and every improper action or morality.

We read in a hadith: "لا تعطى تلتمس اكثر منها" (Do not give seeking more in return), meaning, do not bestow something upon others while expecting a greater return. And we read in another hadith: "لا تستكثر ما عملت من خیرٍ للّه" (Do not consider great the good you have done for Allah), meaning, never consider the good deed you have performed for Allah to be great.(110)

In the previous Surah (Al-Muzzammil), the program of night worship and the relationship with Allah was discussed: "قم اللّیل" (Arise [for] the night). In this Surah, the daily program and the relationship with people are discussed: "قم فانذر" (Arise and warn).

The recitation of "اللّه اكبر" (Allahu Akbar - Allah is Greater) is a clear manifestation of acting upon this verse: "و ربّك فكبّر" (And your Lord magnify).

Magnifying Allah means belittling everything other than Him. Just as the higher an airplane ascends, the smaller the earth and everything upon it appears, so too, for the person for whom Allah is great and who says Allahu Akbar with his entire being, the world is small in his eyes, and he fears no superpower.(111)

As we read among the characteristics of the pious (muttaqīn) in the Sermon of Hammam in Nahj al-Balāghah: "عظم الخالق فى انفسهم فصغر ما دون ذلك" (The Creator is magnified in their souls, so everything besides Him is belittled).

The greatness of Allah is absolute, greater and superior to all things seen, heard, written, thought, described, and so on...

Several points are effective in guidance and warning: Outer purity: "وثیابك فطهر" (And your garments purify); Spiritual purity: "و الرجز فاهجر" (And uncleanness shun); Sincerity in action: "و لا تمنن تستكثر" (And do not bestow favor seeking increase); Patience and perseverance: "و لربّك فاصبر" (And for your Lord be patient).

109) Kāfī, Vol. 6, p. 455. 110) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 111) Imam Khomeini (r.a.)—because he believed in Allahu Akbar—used to say: America cannot do a damn thing.

Messages

  1. All states of the Prophet are subject to Allah’s special attention and grace. "یا ایهّا المدّثّر" (O you wrapped up [in garments]).
  2. The condition for the salvation of the people is that religious leaders must move, abandoning isolation and rest. "قم فانذر" (Arise and warn).
  3. Although the Prophets both give glad tidings and warn, in order to remove heedlessness and awaken the people, warning is necessary, not glad tidings. "فانذر" (So warn).
  4. Humanity is exposed to various dangers, and the task of the Prophets is to warn mankind about all kinds of worldly and otherworldly dangers. "فانذر" (So warn).
  5. Arise with your entire being to carry out divine missions. "قم فانذر" (Arise and warn).
  6. The uprising of the Prophets stems from the command of revelation, not from seeking leadership, superiority, or carnal desires. "قم فانذر" (Arise and warn).
  7. Magnify Allah both in your heart and soul, and magnify Him with your tongue. "ربّك فكبّر" (Your Lord magnify).
  8. Whoever considers Allah great sees his enemy, their tricks, their stings, and their harms as small. "و ربّك فكبّر" (And your Lord magnify).
  9. Greatness belongs exclusively to Allah, not to others. (The word "ربّك" [Your Lord] precedes "فكبّر" [magnify], indicating exclusivity.)
  10. The condition for the heavenly leader's words to penetrate is that his skirt (reputation/conduct) must be pure from all defilement. "و ثیابك فطهّر" (And your garments purify).
  11. Cleanliness and purity, alongside Monotheism (Tawhīd) and magnification (Takbīr), indicate the religion's attention to cleanliness. "و ربّك فكبّر و ثیابك فطهّر" (And your Lord magnify, and your garments purify).
  12. Not only the purity of the body, but also the cleanliness and purity of clothing is given attention in Islam. "و ثیابك فطهّر" (And your garments purify).
  13. The leader of the Islamic society must shun every kind of impurity so that he can be a model and example for everyone. "والرّجز فاهجر" (And uncleanness shun).
  14. Self-purification precedes societal reform. Whoever wishes to cleanse society of defilement must himself be pure from all defilement. "والرّجز فاهجر" (And uncleanness shun).
  15. Neither impose favor upon Allah through worship and spending, counting your deeds as great, nor impose favor upon Allah’s creation. "و لا تمنن تستكثر" (And do not bestow favor seeking increase).
  16. Imposing favor and having expectations in spending and giving are instances of spiritual impurity. "والرجز فاهجر و لا تمنن تستكثر" (And uncleanness shun, and do not bestow favor seeking increase).
  17. Avoiding the imposition of favor and the counting of deeds as excessive is one of the conditions for the success of leaders and preachers. "قم فانذر... و لا تمنن تستكثر" (Arise and warn... and do not bestow favor seeking increase).
  18. The religious leader should not have expectations from the people. "و لا تمنن تستكثر" (And do not bestow favor seeking increase).
  19. Only patience and steadfastness that are for Allah are valuable; otherwise, it is obstinacy and stubbornness. "ولربّك فاصبر" (And for your Lord be patient).
  20. Perseverance is the means of human growth. "ولربّك فاصبر" (And for your Lord be patient).
  21. Although patience is necessary in the face of sin, calamity, and obedience, guiding people requires special patience. "قم فانذر... ولربّك فاصبر" (Arise and warn... And for your Lord be patient).
  22. The resistance of the leader of the Islamic society must be comprehensive: patience against accusations, harms, and denials, and steadfastness in propagation, warning, and worship. "قم... فاصبر" (Arise... be patient).
  23. Inviting people requires serious resolve. "فانذر... فاصبر" (So warn... be patient).

Ayahs

فَإِذَا نُقِرَ فِى ٱلنَّاقُورِ (۸)
When the Trumpet will be sounded, (8)
فَذَٰلِكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوْمٌ عَسِيرٌ (۹)
that will be a day of hardship, (9)
عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ غَيْرُ يَسِيرٍ (۱٠)
not at all easy for the faithless. (10)
ذَرْنِى وَمَنْ خَلَقْتُ وَحِيدًا (۱۱)
Leave Me [to deal] with him whom I created alone, (11)
وَجَعَلْتُ لَهُۥ مَالًا مَّمْدُودًا (۱۲)
and furnished him with extensive means, (12)
وَبَنِينَ شُهُودًا (۱۳)
[gave him] sons to be at his side, (13)
وَمَهَّدتُّ لَهُۥ تَمْهِيدًا (۱٤)
and facilitated [all matters] for him. (14)

Key Points

Naqur (ناقور) is something that is struck to create sound, like a drum, or blown into, like a trumpet. What is meant by it here is the blowing of the Horn (Sur), as He states in other verses: «فاذا نفخ فى الصور» (112) The beak of birds is called minqar (منقار) because it is a tool for striking and piercing, and the sound of the trumpet seems to pierce the ear and penetrate the brain. The Qur'an explicitly mentions two blowings of the Horn: the first blowing, as a result of which everyone dies, and the second blowing, as a result of which everyone is brought back to life.

Near the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) there was a center called Dar al-Nadwah, where the polytheists gathered for consultation. Walid ibn Mughirah, who was a prominent figure known for his intellect and insight, said in a session at that center: Let us unify our statements to confront Muhammad and give a single answer to the pilgrims of the Ka'bah regarding Muhammad. One person said: We will say: Muhammad is a poet. Another said: We will say: He is a soothsayer (kahin). A third said: We will say: He is mad (majnun). Walid frowned and said: We have heard many poems, but Muhammad's speech bears no resemblance to poetry, nor is there any sign of madness in him, and his words are different from those of soothsayers. Finally, they decided to say: Muhammad is a sorcerer (sahhir) and causes separation among family members, such that even within one house, those who believe in him become separated from the rest of the family members.

In these verses, Allah says concerning Walid: I created him and gave him abundant wealth and children, but he misused his intellect and ability and stood against My Prophet. So, woe unto him.

The meaning of wahidan (وحیداً) in «خلقت وحیداً» (I created him alone) is one of the following meanings: 1- Wahid is an attribute of the created being, meaning he was single, alone, weak, and poor, and I bestowed blessings upon him. 2- Wahid is an attribute of the Creator, meaning I created him alone, and now I Myself will hold him accountable alone.

(112) Mu'minun, 101 and Al-Haqqah, 13.

Messages

1- The end of this world and the beginning of the next world will be with a terrifying and mighty sound. «نقر فى النّاقور»

2- The Day of Judgment is extremely difficult for the disbelievers, and even with the passage of time, the disbelieving person will not become accustomed to those hardships, nor will it become easy for him. «عسیر... غیر یسیر»

3- Allah consoles His Prophet, saying: Leave the enemy to Me. «ذرنى...»

4- Both the origin of creation and the affairs of life are in the hand of Allah. «خلقت... جعلت»

5- If wealth and children surround us today, let us not become afflicted by arrogance, and let us not forget that one day we came alone and we will depart alone. «خلقت وحیداً»

6- Human possessions depend on the will and decree of Allah; do not consider the bestowed blessings to be the result of your own knowledge and skill. «جعلت... مهّدت»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ يَطْمَعُ أَنْ أَزِيدَ (۱٥)
Still he is eager that I should give him more. (15)
كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا عَنِيدًا (۱٦)
No indeed! He is an obstinate opponent of Our signs. (16)
سَأُرْهِقُهُۥ صَعُودًا (۱۷)
Soon I will overwhelm him with hardship. (17)
إِنَّهُۥ فَكَّرَ وَقَدَّرَ (۱۸)
Indeed he reflected and decided. (18)
فَقُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ (۱۹)
Perish he, how he decided! (19)
ثُمَّ قُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ (۲٠)
Again, perish he, how he decided! (20)
ثُمَّ نَظَرَ (۲۱)
Then he looked; (21)
ثُمَّ عَبَسَ وَبَسَرَ (۲۲)
then frowned and scowled. (22)
ثُمَّ أَدْبَرَ وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ (۲۳)
Then he walked away disdainfully, (23)
فَقَالَ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ يُؤْثَرُ (۲٤)
saying, ‘It is nothing but traditional sorcery. (24)
إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا قَوْلُ ٱلْبَشَرِ (۲٥)
It is nothing but the speech of a human.’ (25)

Key Points

‘Anīd’ is derived from ‘inād’ (obstinacy), meaning the disbelievers understood the truth, but due to obstinacy and stubbornness, they are unwilling to accept it. ‘Urhīqa’ is derived from ‘irhāq,’ meaning to compel someone to perform a difficult task. ‘Sa‘ūd’ means a steep ascent or mountain pass that is difficult to traverse and climb. The meaning of ‘qaddara’ (He measured/determined) is ‘taqdīr’ (estimation/comparison), referring to the comparison of the Quran with magic, poetry, and soothsaying.

The Quran uses the word ‘qutila’ (may he be destroyed/slain) once regarding disbelief and falsehood: «قتل الانسان ما اكفره» (113) May man (the faithless) be destroyed! How ungrateful he is! «قتل الخرّاصون» (114) May the liars be destroyed. But concerning the conspiracy against the Prophet and the Quran, He stated it twice: «فقتل كیف قدّر ثم قتل كیف قدّر»

In Ayah 23, He said: «ثمّ ادبر واستكبر» (Then he turned away and was arrogant). Now let us see what the punishment is for someone who turns his back on the truth and opposes it.

Regarding those who disbelieve in the truth despite recognizing it and turn away from it, the Quran states that such people suffer various punishments, including:

They lose their direction and purpose in life. «استهوته الشیّطان فى الارض حیران» (115) The deception and temptation of Satan have left him wandering confusedly on earth.

Constriction of the chest puts him under pressure: «یجعل صدره ضیّقاً حرجاً» (116) Allah makes his heart narrow and constricted.

He suffers a difficult and constricted livelihood: «و من اعرض عن ذكرى فانّ له معیشةً ضنكاً» (117) Whoever turns away from My remembrance and obedience, his livelihood shall be constricted.

Misfortune and wretchedness dominate him: «انّ الخزى والسّوء الیوم على الكافرین» (118) Today, disgrace and the evil of the punishment shall be upon the disbelievers.

He is deprived of utilizing divine blessings in the correct way. The Quran states: Allah seals the hearts, ears, and eyes of the disbelievers and casts a veil over them so that they remain deprived of understanding divine truths: «ختم اللّه على قلوبهم و على سمعهم و على ابصارهم غشاوة» (119)

His deeds are like a mirage that a person sees in the desert—hollow and empty: «اعمالهم كسراب بقیعة» (120)

And they are like ashes exposed to a violent gale, all scattered to the wind of annihilation: «اعمالهم كرمادٍ اشتدت به الرّیح فى یومٍ عاصف» (121)

His loss increases every moment: «و لا یزید الكافرین كفرهم الّا خساراً» (122)

He constantly strays far from the path of truth: «ضلّوا ضلالاً بعیداً» (123)

To the extent that he has no other path except Hell and the Fire: «ولا لیهدیهم طریقاً الّا طریق جهنم» (124)

Amīr al-Mu’minīn Ali عليه السلام criticizes the Muslims in Nahj al-Balaghah, saying that your enemy acts with thought and planning, but you are asleep and heedless: «تكادون و لا تكیدون... لا ینام علیكم و انتم فى غفلة» (125)

Obstinate and stubborn people usually have harsh reactions and completely lose their moderation. Although ‘abasa’ (frowned) and ‘basara’ (scowled) have nearly the same meaning, the inclusion of both words indicates their severity and harshness. ‘Abasa and basara’ (frowned and scowled), just as turning away is based on arrogance, here too, both words ‘idbār’ (turning away) and ‘istikbār’ (arrogance) are mentioned. «ثمّ ادبر و استكبر»

113) Abasa, 17 114) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 10 115) Al-An‘ām, 71. 116) Al-An‘ām, 125. 117) Tā Hā, 123. 118) An-Nahl, 27. 119) Al-Baqarah, 7. 120) An-Nūr, 39. 121) Ibrāhīm, 18. 122) Fāṭir, 39. 123) An-Nisā’, 167. 124) An-Nisā’, 169. 125) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 34.

Messages

  1. A person far from Allah is greedy and seeks excess. «یطمع ان ازید»
  2. The enemy does not achieve all his desires. «یطمع ان ازید كلاّ...»
  3. Respond firmly and decisively to undue expectations. «یطمع... كلاّ»
  4. When obstinacy and stubbornness settle in a person, he denies the divine signs. «كان لایاتنا عنیداً»
  5. Obstinacy and stubbornness cause the removal of blessings. «یطمع ان ازید كلا انّه كان لآیاتنا عنیداً»
  6. Obstinacy leads to unbearable torment. «عنیداً سارهقه صعوداً»
  7. Careless prosperity and enjoyment in this world lead to severe hardships on the Day of Judgment. (Indeed, every descent is followed by a steep ascent.) «جعلت له مالاً ممدوداً... سارهقه صعوداً»
  8. The enemy works with thought and planning. «انّه فكّر و قدّر».
  9. The enemy's plan is to fight the Quran and the divine signs. «كان لایاتنا عنیداً... فكّر و قدّر»
  10. Not every thought is valuable, and not every thinker is worthy. «انّه فكّر و قدّر فقتل كیف قدّر»
  11. Conspirators should know that future generations will also curse them. «فقتل كیف قدّر»
  12. Cursing and invoking damnation upon the enemy is not only permissible but necessary. «فقتل كیف قدّر ثمّ قتل كیف قدّر»
  13. Those who inflict cultural damage through their thoughts and schemes, and corrupt leaders, must always be cursed. «فقتل كیف قدّر ثمّ قتل كیف قدّر»
  14. Evil thought is a prelude to a dangerous and incorrect decision. (In the noble Ayah, instead of saying «فقتل كیف فكّر» (May he be destroyed how he thought), He said twice: «قتل كیف قدّر» (May he be destroyed how he determined/measured).)
  15. A moment of thought can be the prelude to good or evil deeds lasting for long periods. The word «فكّر» (thought) is mentioned once, and the word «قدّر» (determined/measured) is repeated three times.
  16. The enemy deepens and sustains his thought and scheme against religion through observation and evaluation. «انّه فكّر و قدّر... ثمّ نظر»
  17. Do not expect smiles and pleasantries from the enemy. «ثمّ عَبس و بَسر»
  18. The enemy spends long periods and exercises patience for his success. «ثمّ نظر، ثمّ عبس، ثمّ ادبر» (The word «ثُمّ» (then) is usually used in cases where there is a significant time interval.)
  19. The Quran, even in the view of the disbelievers, is a highly attractive and magical speech. «ان هذا الاّ سحر یؤثر»

Ayahs

سَأُصْلِيهِ سَقَرَ (۲٦)
Soon I will cast him into Saqar. (26)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا سَقَرُ (۲۷)
And what will show you what is Saqar? (27)
لَا تُبْقِى وَلَا تَذَرُ (۲۸)
It neither spares, nor leaves [anything]. (28)
لَوَّاحَةٌ لِّلْبَشَرِ (۲۹)
It burns the skin. (29)
عَلَيْهَا تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ (۳٠)
There are nineteen [keepers] over it. (30)

Key Points

"Saqar" (سقر) is one of the names of Hell (Dūzakh) and originally means melting due to the heat of the sun. (126) "Lawwāḥ" (لوّاح) and "Talwīḥ" (تلویح) mean changing color to blackness or redness. "Bishar" (بَشر) is the plural of "Bushrah" (بُشرة), meaning the outer surface of the skin. It is narrated in history that when Abu Jahl heard that Hell has nineteen guardians (mā'mūr), he told his companions: Let every ten of you overpower one of them. One of the strong men of Quraysh said: I will overpower seventeen of them, and you two overpower the rest. (127) Some have said that the intent of the Ayah "لا تبقى و لا تذر" (It spares nothing and leaves nothing) is that the fire of Hell neither kills nor keeps alive, similar to the Ayah "لا یموت فیها و لا یحیى" (He neither dies therein nor lives) (128) and similar to the Ayah "كلّما نضجت جلودهم بدّلناهم جلوداً" (Every time their skins are roasted through, We shall change them for fresh skins). (129)

126) Mufradāt Rāghib. 127) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān. 128) Al-A‘lā, 13. 129) An-Nisā’, 59.

Messages

1- The reality of Hell (Dūzakh) cannot be comprehended except through revelation. "و ما ادراك ما سقر" (And what will make you know what Saqar is?)

2- The fire of Hell is both comprehensive: "لا تُبقى" (It spares nothing), and unquenchable: "لا تذر" (and leaves nothing).

3- The Resurrection (Ma‘ād) is physical. "لوّاحةٌ للبشر" (Scorching the human skin).

4- The punishment for one who misrepresents Divine revelation and presents it to people in the guise of magic is the transformation of his body's skin by the fire of Hell. "لوّاحة للبشر" (Scorching the human skin).

5- If one angel can take the lives of all people, and if two angels can overturn the city of Lot and destroy all its inhabitants, then nineteen angels can be responsible for the torment of all criminals. "علیها تسعه عشر" (Over it are nineteen).

Ayah

وَمَا جَعَلْنَآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلنَّارِ إِلَّا مَلَـٰٓئِكَةً ۙ وَمَا جَعَلْنَا عِدَّتَهُمْ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِيَسْتَيْقِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَيَزْدَادَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِيمَـٰنًا ۙ وَلَا يَرْتَابَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۙ وَلِيَقُولَ ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ مَاذَآ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَـٰذَا مَثَلًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَا يَعْلَمُ جُنُودَ رَبِّكَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَمَا هِىَ إِلَّا ذِكْرَىٰ لِلْبَشَرِ (۳۱)
We have assigned only angels as keepers of the Fire, and We have made their number merely a stumbling block for the faithless, and so that those who were given the Book may be reassured, and the faithful may increase in [their] faith, and so that those who were given the Book and the faithful may not be in doubt, and so that the faithless and those in whose hearts is a sickness may say, ‘What did Allah mean by this description?’ Thus does Allah lead astray whomever He wishes and guides whomever He wishes. No one knows the hosts of your Lord except Him, and it is just an admonition for all humans. (31)

Key Points

Fakhr Razi says in his Tafsir Kabir: The number 19, which is the number of the guardians of Hellfire, leads to certainty for the People of the Book and the Muslims in several ways: A) Since this very number appeared in previous heavenly books, namely the Torah and the Gospel, the People of the Book said: How did an unlettered person become aware of it? Therefore, revelation must certainly be sent down to him. B) The faith of the People of the Book in the truthfulness of the Quran causes the faith of the Muslims to increase. C) The fact that the number of the guardians of Hellfire is 19 causes ridicule and astonishment for some, and normally a person does not say something that people would laugh at. Now that the Quran has mentioned this number as the count of the guardians of Hellfire, it becomes clear that this speech is from revelation.

In some verses of the Quran, the misguidance of people is attributed to Allah, similar to the end of this very verse where He says: «یضلّ اللّه من یشاء» (Allah leaves astray whom He wills). However, we must know that the intent of idhlal (misguidance) is to abandon someone to their own devices. That is, Allah leaves the unworthy person to their own state. This is like a farmer who abandons and sets aside a rotten and spoiled seed or pit, but keeps the healthy seed and provides the means for its growth.

Studying the Torah and the Gospel and comparing them with the Quran is one of the ways to increase the faith of the believers. This is because they both discover the distorted parts of those two previous books and encounter many common points that prove the truthfulness of the Quran.

We must maintain a respectful attitude towards religious rulings and matters, and never insult, belittle, or ridicule things that we find astonishing.

Stating that the number of the guardians of Hellfire is nineteen is a means of testing who accepts it and who takes it in ridicule.

The administration and management of the affairs of existence are extremely precise and calculated. The agents of the cosmic system are placed in their positions according to the required number—neither one person more nor one person less. Based on Divine knowledge, 19 angels are sufficient to manage Hellfire and its inhabitants, so not a single person is added. This is the best lesson for managers: that they should hire staff only to the extent of need and necessity.

Messages

1- The guardians of Hellfire are angels. «ما جعلنا اصحاب النار الا ملائكة»

2- Only someone who is not polluted himself and is not afflicted by what others have been forbidden can be the executor of Divine laws and commands. «و ما جعلنا اصحاب النار الا ملائكة»

3- Mentioning the number of the guardians of Hellfire is for the testing of the disbelievers. «الاّ فتنة للذین كفروا»

4- There are also fair-minded individuals among the People of the Book who, upon seeing the truthfulness of Islam, embrace faith. «لیستیقن الّذین اوتوا الكتاب...»

5- The Quran informs about the future speech of the fault-finding sick-hearted. «و لیقول الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض...»

6- Allah's misguidance is not initial, but rather a punishment for the disbelievers. (Like a thief who enters a house and you close the door on him. Here, imprisoning the thief has a punitive aspect.) «یضلّ اللّه من یشاء»

7- Disbelievers and hypocrites are coordinated in their enmity towards Islam and Muslims. «ولیقول الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض والكافرون »

8- Sometimes a simple matter guides some individuals and misguides others. «ماذا اراد اللّه بهذا مثلاً كذلك یضلّ اللّه من یشاء و یهدى من یشاء»

Ayahs

كَلَّا وَٱلْقَمَرِ (۳۲)
No indeed! By the Moon! (32)
وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذْ أَدْبَرَ (۳۳)
By the night when it recedes! (33)
وَٱلصُّبْحِ إِذَآ أَسْفَرَ (۳٤)
By the dawn when it brightens! (34)
إِنَّهَا لَإِحْدَى ٱلْكُبَرِ (۳٥)
Indeed they are one of the greatest [signs of God] (35)
نَذِيرًا لِّلْبَشَرِ (۳٦)
—a warner to all humans, (36)
لِمَن شَآءَ مِنكُمْ أَن يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوْ يَتَأَخَّرَ (۳۷)
[alike] for those of you who like to advance ahead and those who would remain behind. (37)

Key Points

«Asfara» (أَسْفَرَ) is derived from «safara» (سَفَرَ), meaning to unveil or make manifest. Unveiled women are called «sāfirāt» (سافرات). «Kubar» (كُبَر) is the plural of «kubrā» (كُبرى), meaning greater. This means that the punishment mentioned in the previous verses is only one of the great Divine punishments, and there are other great punishments as well. The moon, due to its movement around the Earth, providing light at night, its beauty, its function as a natural calendar, its creation of tides, and its other roles in existence, is one of the great signs of Allah that deserves to be sworn by. Allah has sworn by all parts of time once in the Quran, but He swears by the time of dawn (Sahar) three times. «Wal-Fajr» (130), «Waṣ-Ṣubḥ» (131), «Waḍ-Ḍuḥā» (132), and «Wal-ʿAṣr» (133) each appear once. However, when the night ends and dawn approaches, it is mentioned with three expressions: «Wal-Layli idh adbara» (134), «Wal-Layli idhā yasri» (135), and «Wal-Layli idhā ʿasʿasa» (136). Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: Whoever hastens towards accepting our Wilayah (guardianship) will be distanced from Hellfire, and whoever lags behind in accepting our Wilayah will hasten towards Hellfire. (137) «liman shā’a minkum an yataqaddama aw yata’akhkhara»

(130) Al-Fajr, 1. (131) Al-Muddaththir, 34. (132) Ad-Duha, 1. (133) Al-Asr, 1. (134) Al-Muddaththir, 33. (135) Al-Fajr, 4. (136) At-Takwir, 17. (137) Tafsir Nur Ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The creations of Allah in nature possess such importance that they are worthy of being sworn by. «Wal-Qamari, Wal-Layli... Waṣ-Ṣubḥi»
  2. The warnings of the Prophet of Islam are for all humanity. «Nadhīran lil-bashar»
  3. Warning is necessary for their guidance. «Nadhīran lil-bashar»
  4. Man possesses free will and choice. «liman shā’a»
  5. The religious person is ahead of others, and the irreligious person is lagging behind. «liman shā’a minkum an yataqaddama aw yata’akhkhara»

Ayahs

كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ رَهِينَةٌ (۳۸)
Every soul is hostage to what it has earned, (38)
إِلَّآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۳۹)
except the People of the Right Hand. (39)
فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (٤٠)
[They will be] in gardens, questioning (40)
عَنِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (٤۱)
the guilty: (41)
مَا سَلَكَكُمْ فِى سَقَرَ (٤۲)
‘What drew you into Hell?’ (42)
قَالُوا۟ لَمْ نَكُ مِنَ ٱلْمُصَلِّينَ (٤۳)
They will answer, ‘We were not among those who prayed. (43)
وَلَمْ نَكُ نُطْعِمُ ٱلْمِسْكِينَ (٤٤)
Nor did we feed the poor. (44)
وَكُنَّا نَخُوضُ مَعَ ٱلْخَآئِضِينَ (٤٥)
We used to indulge in [profane] gossip along with the gossipers, (45)
وَكُنَّا نُكَذِّبُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (٤٦)
and we used to deny the Day of Retribution (46)
حَتَّىٰٓ أَتَىٰنَا ٱلْيَقِينُ (٤۷)
until death came to us.’ (47)

Key Points

The intent of the Companions of the Right (Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn) is the same group mentioned in Ayah 27 of Sūrat al-Wāqi‘ah: «و اصحاب الیمین ما اصحاب الیمین فى سدر مخضود...» Those whose record of deeds will be given to their right hand on the Day of Judgment. On the Day of Judgment, there will be many bitter and sweet conversations, greetings or curses, questioning and reprimand, blaming sins and crimes on others, and mutual cursing. The Qur'an mentions conversations between believers and believers, criminals and criminals, criminals and believers, believers and criminals, and also angels with believers and criminals.

The intent of feeding the needy (iṭ‘ām al-miskīn) is either the obligatory Zakat, the abandonment of which necessitates Hellfire, or it means that even if Zakat has been paid, but a hungry person exists in society, feeding him is obligatory, and indifference towards him leads to entering the Hellfire. Perhaps the intent of feeding the needy includes all his needs, such as clothing and shelter, not just food and sustenance.

Both denying the Day of Judgment is a prelude to sin: «ارأیت الذى یكذّب بالدین فذلك الذّى یدعّ الیتیم»(138), and sin leads man towards disbelief and denial: «ثمّ كان عاقبة الّذین اساؤا السواى اَن كذّبوا»(139). Therefore, the Ayah «و كنّا نكذّب بیوم الدین» can be both the cause of the criminals' actions mentioned in the previous verses and the result of their sins.

The intent of "certainty" (al-yaqīn) in this Ayah is death, either because it is the most certain matter for everyone, and no one doubts it, or because at the moment of death, all truths are revealed to man, and everyone reaches the degree of certainty and belief. «حتى اتانا الیقین»

A prayer (Ṣalāh) that is performed occasionally does not place a person among the prayer performers. «لم نك من المصلّین» means our prayer lacked continuity. It is also stated in Sūrat al-Mā‘ūn: «فویل للمصلّین الّذینهم عن صلاتهم ساهون»(140), and such a prayer does not make a person eligible for the intercession of the intercessors. «لم نك من المصلّین... فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین»(141) And this is the famous tradition of Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, who, on the verge of his martyrdom, asked for his relatives to be present and addressed them, saying: «لا ینال شفاعتنا من استخف بالصّلاة»(142) (Our intercession will not reach those who take the prayer lightly).

People are either fā’iz (successful) or khā’iḍ (engaging in vain talk); meaning they either achieve success, prosperity, and salvation, in which case they are fā’iz, or they delve into and immerse themselves in vanity and falsehood, in which case they are khā’iḍ.

Qurtubi, one of the famous Sunni commentators, narrated from Imam Baqir عليه السلام that He said: We and our followers (Shi‘ah) are the Companions of the Right, and whoever harbors malice and hatred towards us, the Ahl al-Bayt, is held in pledge. «نحن و شیعتنا اصحاب الیمین و كلّ من ابغضنا اهل البیت فهم المرتهنون»(143)

138) Ma‘ūn, 2 and 3. 139) Rūm, 10. 140) Ma‘ūn, 5 and 6. 141) Mudaththir, 43 and 48. 142) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 270. 143) Tafsīr Qurtubī.

Messages

1- Man's behavior shapes his character. «كلّ نفس بما كسبت رهینة»

2- Man's salvation on the Day of Judgment and his release from divine punishment depend on answering the questions in Allah's court of justice. «كلّ نفس بما كسبت رهینة»

3- Asking successive questions of the criminals on the Day of Judgment is a form of psychological torment for them. «یتساءلون عن المجرمین»

4- No one goes to Hellfire for a single sin; persistence and continuity in sin and corruption are the cause of entering the Hellfire. (The phrases «لم نك» and «كنّا» indicate the continuity of the action.)

5- Feeding the needy is necessary, even if he is not a Muslim. «لم نك نطعم المسكین»

6- Spending one's life in futility is an unforgivable crime. «كنّا نخوض مع الخائفین»

7- One of the factors leading to Hellfire is conforming to corrupt company. «كنّا نخوض مع الخائضین»

8- Bad friends and companions pave the way to Hellfire. «كنّا نخوض مع الخائضین»

9- Death is not annihilation, but rather liberation from the captivity of the material world and the viewing of transcendental truths. «كنّا نكذّب بیوم الدین حتّى اتانا الیقین»

Ayahs

فَمَا تَنفَعُهُمْ شَفَـٰعَةُ ٱلشَّـٰفِعِينَ (٤۸)
So the intercession of the intercessors will not avail them. (48)
فَمَا لَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلتَّذْكِرَةِ مُعْرِضِينَ (٤۹)
What is the matter with them that they evade the Reminder (49)
كَأَنَّهُمْ حُمُرٌ مُّسْتَنفِرَةٌ (٥٠)
as if they were terrified asses (50)
فَرَّتْ مِن قَسْوَرَةٍۭ (٥۱)
fleeing from a lion? (51)
بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُؤْتَىٰ صُحُفًا مُّنَشَّرَةً (٥۲)
But everyone of them desires to be given unrolled scriptures [from Allah]! (52)
كَلَّا ۖ بَل لَّا يَخَافُونَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ (٥۳)
No! Indeed, they do not fear the Hereafter. (53)
كَلَّآ إِنَّهُۥ تَذْكِرَةٌ (٥٤)
No! It is indeed a reminder. (54)
فَمَن شَآءَ ذَكَرَهُۥ (٥٥)
So let anyone who wishes be mindful of it. (55)

Key Points

«حُمُر» is the plural of «حِمار», referring to the wild donkey, and its Persian equivalent is gūr-khar (onager/wild ass). The word gūr means desert, and gūr-khar means desert donkey. In the simile comparing the flight of the disbelievers from the Quran to the flight of the onager from the lion, several points are hidden: A) The onager's flight from the lion is not based on intellect or consciousness. B) This flight is accompanied by terror. C) The flight does not occur with a specific goal. D) The fugitive lacks the strength or opportunity to recuperate and will ultimately be captured. «قَسورة» derives from «قَسر», meaning coercion and dominance, and is one of the names for a lion. In some contexts, it also means hunter. It seems the intent is the flight of the onager from the lion or the hunter.

In some narrations, it is mentioned that Abu Jahl and a group from Quraysh told the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله: We will not believe in you unless you bring a letter from the heavens whose title is: From the Lord of the Worlds to so-and-so son of so-and-so, and in it, we are officially commanded to believe in you. (144)

In some verses of the Holy Quran, mention is made of the inappropriate expectation of the disbelievers who said: «و لن نؤمن لرقیك حتّى تنزل علینا كتاباً نقرؤه» (145) We will never believe in you unless you send down upon us a book that we may read.

In Surah Al-An'am, we also read a verse similar to Ayah 52, where they said: «لن نؤمن حتّى نؤتى مثل ما اوتى رسول اللّه» (146) We will not believe unless we are given the like of what was given to the Messenger of Allah.

In response to these inappropriate expectations, Allah says: «اللّه اعلم حیث یجعل رسالته» (147) Allah knows best where to place His message.

Intercessors on the Day of Judgment

We read in the Quran and narrations that Allah will permit certain individuals on the Day of Judgment to intercede for others.

In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, we read: «انا اوّل شافع» (148) I am the first intercessor (for My community).

The Quran. Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام says: On the Day of Judgment, the Quran is among the intercessors. (149)

The Prophets. It is mentioned in narrations that the Prophets will intercede on the Day of Judgment: «یشفع الانبیاء» (150)

The Infallible Imams and true Shi'a. In a narration from an Infallible Imam عليه السلام, we read: On the Day of Judgment, intercession will be for us and for our Shi'a. «لنا شفاعة و لاهل مودّتنا شفاعة» (151)

The Angels. On the Day of Judgment, the Angels and the martyrs will be permitted to intercede (for whomever they wish): «یؤذن للملائكة و الشهداء ان یشفعوا» (152)

The Martyrs. On the Day of Judgment, every martyr will intercede for seventy members of his family. (153)

Acts of Worship. The acts of worship performed by servants will be among the means of intercession on the Day of Judgment: «الصیام والقرآن شفیعان للعبد یوم القیامة» (154)

Conditions for those who receive intercession

The Quran and narrations identify those who qualify for intercession as having the following conditions:

A) They must be people of faith, prayer, and spending in the way of Allah, and they must not have wasted their lives. «فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین» B) Allah must have granted permission for their intercession. «من ذا الذى یشفع عنده الاّ باذنه» (155) C) They must die with faith. D) There must be a relationship between the intercessor and the one being interceded for. Anyone who lacks a doctrinal connection with the intercessors will not be included in the intercession.

144) Tafsir Maraghi. 145) Al-Isra, 93. 146) Al-An'am, 124. 147) Al-An'am, 125. 148) Sahih Muslim, Vol. 2, p. 130. 149) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 176. 150) Musnad Ahmad, Vol. 3, p. 12. 151) Khisal, p. 624. 152) Musnad Ahmad, Vol. 5, p. 43. 153) Sunan Abi Dawud, Vol. 3, p. 15. 154) Musnad Ahmad, Vol. 2, p. 174. 155) Al-Baqarah, 255.

Messages

1- Negligence in prayer, charity, feeding the needy, and wasting one's life lead to deprivation of intercession on the Day of Judgment. «فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین ...»

2- Allah will permit certain individuals on the Day of Judgment to intercede for others, but many people do not meet the conditions for receiving intercession. «فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعین»

3- On the Day of Judgment, the intercessors are numerous: Prophets, Successors (of the Prophets), martyrs, the Quran, and others. «الشافعین»

4- Opponents of Islam have no reason for rejecting the divine verses and the word of revelation. «فما لهم عن التذكرة معرضین»

5- Those who turn away from the truth lack the benefit of intellect and wisdom. «كانّهم حمر»

6- Having inappropriate expectations from Allah (such as requesting a letter from Him) is a sign of foolishness. «كانهم حُمر ... یرید كلّ امرء منهم...»

7- The disbelievers are arrogant and expect that a special invitation letter should be sent for the guidance of each one of them to the path of truth. «بل یرید كلّ امرء منهم ان یؤتى صحفاً منشّرة»

8- Respond decisively with a negative answer to inappropriate expectations. (Allah has completed the argument by sending proof and miracles, and there is no need to send private letters.) «كلاّ»

9- The inappropriate demands of the disbelievers for believing are nothing more than excuses. «یرید... كلاّ»

10- Indifference toward the Day of Judgment is the cause of denying prophethood. «عن التذكرة معرضین ... بل لا یخافون الاخرة»

11- Man is free and possesses will in choosing his belief and ideology. «فمن شاء»

12- The Quran is a suitable platform for the guidance of humanity, but being guided and benefiting from this platform is conditional upon man's own will. «فمن شاء ذكره»

Ayah

وَمَا يَذْكُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ هُوَ أَهْلُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ وَأَهْلُ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ (٥٦)
And they will not be mindful unless Allah wishes. He is worthy of [your] being wary [of Him] and He is worthy to forgive. (56)

Messages

1- Although man possesses free will: «فمن شاء ذكره», his will alone is not sufficient, and nothing happens unless Allah wills it. «و ما یذكرون الّا ان یشاء اللّه»

2- Taking heed from Divine reminders is not possible except through Divine enablement (Tawfiq). «و ما یذكرون الّا ان یشاء اللّه»

3- Stubborn and obstinate disbelievers will not believe at any cost, unless Allah wills and compels them (which Allah does not do). «و ما یذكرون الّا ان یشاء اللّه»

4- Only Allah is worthy that we be mindful of Him (Taqwa), and if we commit a transgression, He alone is the One who can pardon and forgive us. «هو اهل التقوى و اهل المغفرة»
